channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: CISOs Feel the Heat Over AI Risk
item.description: Job pressures have increased as companies run headlong into AI adoption, causing 26% of top security executives to consider leaving their position....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Attackers Combo Up Evasion Tactics for BEC Phishing
item.description: "The TFF Trap" uses fileless techniques and loaders with low detection rates to deploy various RATs and stealers, including Agent Tesla, Remcos, XWorm, and Best Private Logger....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: FakeGit Campaign Uses 7,600 GitHub Repositories to Spread SmartLoader Malware
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered nearly 7,600 malicious GitHub repositories, out of which more than 800 pose as artificial intelligence (AI) skills or Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers to ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New HollowGraph malware uses Microsoft Graph for stealthy C2 comms
item.description: A malicious component dubbed HollowGraph uses the calendar feature in compromised Microsoft 365 mailboxes as a command-and-control channel to receive attacker commands and exfiltrate stolen data. [......
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Exposed Server Reveals AI-Assisted Phishing Toolkit Behind WebDAV Malware Campaign
item.description: A malware operator left its delivery server wide open, and Rapid7 pulled down the whole toolkit: 1,048 files spanning lure templates, filename-spoofing tests, execution experiments, droppers, builder ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: HollowGraph Malware Hides C2 and Stolen Files in Microsoft 365 Events Dated 2050
item.description: A newly discovered espionage implant has been using a hijacked Microsoft 365 calendar as its command channel, planting operator instructions and smuggling out stolen files as attachments on calendar e...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: An AI SOC Evaluation Guide for Security Leaders
item.description: Choosing an AI SOC platform requires understanding how it will perform in your own environment, not just during an evaluation. Prophet Security shares a practical framework for assessing AI SOC soluti...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cybersecurity Keeps Events 'Uneventful'
item.description: From the World Cup to the United States' 250th celebration, this year's event calendar has been packed with high-profile gatherings that drew global audiences, intense scrutiny, and enormous security ...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: WordPress RCE, SonicWall 0-Days, AI Service Attacks, SharePoint 0-Day and More
item.description:
A single request should not be able to do this much. But this week, small inputs led to code execution, memory loss, stolen keys, and disabled security tools.
The paths were often simple: exposed sys...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Russian Intelligence Hacks IP Cameras to Spy on Military Logistics Across NATO States and Ukraine
item.description: At least one Russian intelligence service is systematically hijacking internet-connected security cameras across Europe and Ukraine, using the feeds to watch military transport routes, weapons shipmen...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hugging Face warns an autonomous AI agent hacked its network
item.description: The Hugging Face artificial intelligence repository disclosed that attackers gained access to internal datasets and credentials after breaching its production infrastructure using an autonomous AI age...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Mythos Didn't Break Your Security Program. Your Exposure Window Could.
item.description: The industry spent the initial months after Anthropic's April 7 Mythos reveal focused on volume. How many new CVEs would Mythos add to an already overloaded pipeline? How quickly would the flood of AI...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft confirms Windows Server Update Services sync delays
item.description: Microsoft is working to fix a known issue affecting Windows Server Update Services (WSUS) servers, which has caused synchronization problems for more than a week. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Windows KB5121767 OOB update fixes shutdowns on some Dell PCs
item.description: Microsoft has released emergency updates to fix a known issue causing some Dell PCs to shut down after installing the July 2026 Windows 11 security updates. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical ServiceNow code execution flaw now exploited in attacks
item.description: Attackers have begun exploiting a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-6875) in the ServiceNow AI Platform, according to threat intelligence company Defused. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New 7-Zip Vulnerability Could Let Crafted XZ Archives Run Code During Extraction
item.description: Opening a crafted XZ archive in 7-Zip could let an attacker run code on the machine. The flaw, CVE-2026-14266, is a heap-based buffer overflow in how the archiver processes XZ chunked data, and Trend ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Russian-Speaking Hacker Uses Google Gemini CLI to Control Botnet of Eight Dental Clinic PCs
item.description:
A solo Russian-speaking threat actor known as "bandcampro" outsourced a chunk of their operations to Google's open-source Gemini CLI artificial intelligence (AI) and commandeered a live botnet.
The f...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: World's Largest AI Model Repository Hugging Face Breached by Autonomous AI Agent
item.description:
In an ironic twist, open-source artificial intelligence (AI) platform Hugging Face revealed that it was the victim of a hack perpetrated by an autonomous AI agent system.
The company said it detected...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: SleeperGem Uses Three Malicious RubyGems Packages to Target Developer Machines
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new software supply chain attack codenamed SleeperGem targeting the Ruby ecosystem after three malicious gems were published to RubyGems with the end goal of s...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Critical NGINX Vulnerability Can Crash Workers and May Allow Remote Code Execution
item.description: F5 has shipped fixes for a critical nginx flaw that lets a remote, unauthenticated attacker trigger a heap buffer overflow in the worker process with crafted HTTP requests. CVE-2026-42533 was patched ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hackers abuse ViPNet software to target Russian govt agencies
item.description: An advanced threat actor is abusing the update mechanism for the ViPNet private networking product suite to target Russian organizations, including government agencies. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: UAC-0145 Uses ClickFix CAPTCHAs to Infect Ukrainian Devices wih Malware
item.description:
Russian state-sponsored threat actors have been observed leveraging the infamous ClickFix strategy to trick Ukrainian targets into infecting their own machines with data-stealing malware.
According t...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: SonicWall SMA Zero-Days Exploited Before Disclosure to Gain Root Access
item.description: A previously undocumented threat actor has been attributed to the exploitation of recently disclosed SonicWall Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 series VPN appliances as zero-days prior their public dis...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Update now: 7-Zip fixes RCE flaw exploitable with malicious archives
item.description: 7-Zip version 26.02 was released on June 25 to fix a remote code execution vulnerability that could allow attackers to execute malicious code by convincing users to open specially crafted compressed f...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: WordPress Core "wp2shell" RCE flaws get public exploits, patch now
item.description: Public exploits have been released for the critical "wp2shell" remote code execution vulnerabilities affecting WordPress Core, making it imperative that administrators patch their sites immediately. [...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft warns of surge in ACR Stealer attacks on customers
item.description: Microsoft has observed a surge in attacks using the ACR Stealer malware to steal browser-stored passwords, authentication tokens, and sensitive documents from its enterprise customers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The Future of Age Verification: Your Face Never Leaves Your Device
item.description: As age verification laws expand worldwide, organizations face growing pressure to protect users' privacy while meeting regulatory requirements. Incode explains how on-device age estimation verifies ag...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New wp2shell WordPress Core Flaw Lets Unauthenticated Attackers Run Code
item.description: Updated July 18, 2026: the two flaws now carry CVE IDs, the full mechanism has been published, a persistent-object-cache condition has surfaced, and a working proof-of-concept is public. The story bel...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Abbott probes two cyber incidents amid extortion claims
item.description: Abbott Laboratories is investigating two separate cybersecurity incidents after confirming unauthorized access to internal legacy Exact Sciences systems in its Cancer Diagnostics business, while also ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: OpenSSL HollowByte Flaw Could Freeze Server Memory with 11-Byte TLS Requests
item.description: Eleven bytes will make an unpatched OpenSSL server set aside up to 131 KB of memory for a message that never arrives. On the glibc systems Okta tested, that memory is gone until the process restarts. ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
